etymology.description: 田(田) (tián, "field") as the semantic component + 介(介) (jiè, "mediate") as the phonetic component = indicates a boundary surrounding fields -> boundary; realm
etymology.interpretation: The character 界 (jiè, "boundary") combines the idea of a field (田) with something that mediates or divides (介), which logically conveys the concept of setting boundaries or defining a realm.

examples.chinese: 科学界对这个发现感到兴奋(科學界對這個發現感到興奮)。
examples.pinyin: Kēxué jiè duì zhège fāxiàn gǎndào xīngfèn.
examples.english: The scientific community is excited about this discovery.

examples.chinese: 他在艺术界很有名(他在藝術界很有名)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài yìshù jiè hěn yǒumíng.
examples.english: He is well-known in the art world.

examples.chinese: 这本书探讨了神秘的精神界(這本書探討了神秘的精神界)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū tàntǎo le shénmì de jīngshén jiè.
examples.english: This book explores the mysterious spiritual realm.
